methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
X-Energy, a nuclear energy company founded by Kam Ghaffarian, is approaching IPO with a current valuation of $2B+ and $1.1B+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, similar-valuation-tier companies spanning public equities (Oklo at $10.0B, Nano Nuclear Energy at $1.5B) and late-stage private firms (TerraPower at $4.0B, Kairos Power at $1.6B, Zap Energy at $1.0B). This peer universe represents the core comparable set in nuclear energy ventures currently valued between $1.0B and $10.0B.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $1.6B based on the median across the five comparables. This method anchors to observable market pricing in the same sector and stage cohort, providing direct market validation. The peer range spans $1.0B to $10.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in technology maturity, deployment stage, and regulatory pathway within advanced nuclear.

Triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $1.8B, with a median of $1.6B. The $2B+ current valuation sits modestly above this range. Key uncertainties include regulatory approval timelines, capital deployment efficiency relative to peers, and market differentiation in reactor design versus the broader field. The single-method reliance also limits robustness; revenue or EBITDA multiples would strengthen conviction if available. At IPO, management narrative on these factors will be material to pricing precision.
